seo_description: Current time in Alexandria, Egypt. Eastern European Time (EET, UTC+2) year-round — Egypt does not observe DST. Live clock and time differences for Alexandria.

faq.question: What time is it in Alexandria right now?
faq.answer: Alexandria, Egypt uses Eastern European Time (EET, UTC+2) year-round. Egypt abolished Daylight Saving Time in 2011. The IANA identifier is Africa/Cairo (same as Cairo, as all of Egypt uses the same time zone). Alexandria is Egypt's second-largest city and main port on the Mediterranean. The live clock above shows the current Alexandria time.

faq.question: What time zone is Alexandria, Egypt in?
faq.answer: Alexandria, Egypt uses Eastern European Time (EET, UTC+2) year-round. The IANA identifier is Africa/Cairo. All of Egypt — including Cairo, Alexandria, Luxor, and Aswan — uses the same UTC+2 offset permanently. Egypt abolished Daylight Saving Time in 2011.

faq.question: Does Alexandria observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Alexandria, Egypt does not observe Daylight Saving Time. Egypt permanently abolished DST in 2011 and uses EET (UTC+2) year-round. Previously, Egypt observed DST (EEST, UTC+3) in summer, but this practice ended in 2011.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Alexandria and London?
faq.answer: Alexandria (EET, UTC+2) is 2 hours ahead of London (GMT, UTC+0) in winter. During UK summer time (BST, UTC+1), Alexandria is 1 hour ahead. Egypt does not observe DST.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Alexandria and New York?
faq.answer: Alexandria (EET, UTC+2) is 7 hours ahead of New York (EST, UTC-5) in winter. During US DST (EDT, UTC-4), Alexandria is 6 hours ahead. Egypt does not observe DST.

faq.question: Is Alexandria in the same time zone as Cairo?
faq.answer: Yes. Alexandria and Cairo are in the same time zone — both use Africa/Cairo (EET, UTC+2) year-round. They always share the same time. Alexandria is approximately 225 km (140 miles) northwest of Cairo along the Mediterranean coast.
